increase of the moon, the waxing moon
that is no sleep, from which one easily awakes
bringing news of what oneself has seen
together with the men, men and all
ὁ προεστώς τινος πράγματος καὶ αὐθεντῶν
on the other hand, on the contrary
offering of oneself, of one"s free will
one who has himself heard, ear-witness
forthwith, at once, in a moment
the ideal good, the Form of good
the ideal man, the Form of man
by a mere shout, at the first shout
sprung from the same stock, kindred
in one piece with the point
on one"s own judgement, at one"s own discretion
is of one piece with the
bringing his own share to a feast
with independent jurisdiction, with one"s own courts
the object of opinion in the abstract
from where you stand, not coming nearer
from oneself, of one"s own accord, spontaneously
have the principle of motion in oneself
festival in honour of the Emperor
the ideal circle, the Form of circle
with the very words, in express words
one who carries his own oil-flask, one who has no slave to do so
that plays the lyre to himself
that which happens of itself, chance
acting of one"s own will, of oneself
happening of themselves, without external agency
not to be accounted for externally
fight for oneself, plead one"s own cause
freedom to use its own laws, independence
living under one"s own laws, independent
on one"s own feet, on foot
use of one"s own feet, walking
making not a copy, but the thing itself
not planted by man, naturally grown
made of wool only, all wool
on foot, on one"s own feet
enjoying the privilege of collecting one"s own taxes
together with the root, root and branch
self-acting, acting of free will, Proll.Hermog.
in one"s own person, without a mask
in which the author speaks in his own person
concerned with a direct vision of divinity
in close fight, in the mêlée
near at hand, hand to hand
ending in itself, complete in itself
eating at the same table with
the ideal triangle, the Form of triangle
a piece of one"s own work
able to work with one"s own hand
one who works his land himself
written with one"s own hand, autograph
murder perpetrated by one"s own hand
commit a murder with one"s own hand
of one and the same colour
in this very manner, even so
just as before, as it was
bind the neck with a ligature
say confidently, to be proudly confident
thing boasted of, object of pride, the pride, boast
get a light, light a fire
that which is taken away as the choice part
assertion of freedom of a reputed slave
one must take away in thought
fail to put in an appearance
which can be secreted and made away with
to be hung on, hang on
that which is let go: remission of tribute
exemption from attendance, leave of absence
one who lets off a military engine
sand sprinkled over wrestlers, to enable them to get a grip of one another
to be past the prime of life
to be absent for a day
not liable to perish, undecaying, imperishable
not to be made a friend of
contest of riders who dismounted while racing
put away, remove, keep out of the way
causing no fear, free from fear
look away, have the back turned
mark off for oneself, appropriate to oneself
that which is set apart: the wave-offering
not having undergone the Lycurgean training
quitting oneself of the orders of
eschew on religious grounds, hold in abomination
not to be kept in, irrepressible
least likely to be thought of
belonging to the goddess of love
have no care of, pay no heed to
work off youthful passion, sow one"s wild oats
without natural talent, not clever, dull
can be used, not guarded against
not seconded by the suppliant"s olive-branch
contrary to the laws of nature
not to be explained by science
draw for oneself, help oneself to
with a bad grace, with an ill will
islands at the mouth of the Achelous
anything that comes off the surface
on which no public business was done
to be in want of money
to be colourless: to be discoloured, ill-coloured
not to lie, to speak truth
to be tied in a circle
at an untimely hour, too early
the choicest, the flower of its kind
that which gives honour and glory
